channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Rights groups urge Thailand to release Vietnamese activist, saying he faces torture if extradited
item.description: A group of nearly three dozen rights groups are calling on Thailand’s prime minister to release a Vietnamese activist who has been ordered extradited home to face imprisonment on terrorism charges, saying he faces the possibility of torture if returned.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia flaunts its many doomsday weapons to keep the West from ramping up support for Ukraine
item.description: This year has seen President Vladimir Putin repeatedly brandish the nuclear sword, reminding everyone that Russia has the world’s largest atomic arsenal in order to deter the West from ramping up support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An American was reportedly kidnapped from a southern Philippine town and a search is underway
item.description: Philippine police say a search is underway after four gunmen reportedly abducted an American national, who was shot in the leg as he tried to resist before being spirited away by speedboat.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Argentine authorities probe what happened before Liam Payne's fatal fall from his hotel balcony
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — The circumstances surrounding the death of ex-One Direction singer Liam Payne were suspicious and possibly involved drugs, though there was no sign of a third party being involved, Argentine prosecutors said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Efforts to combat deforestation in Colombia hindered because of armed groups, report finds
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Armed groups operating in Colombia's Amazon are tightening their grip on the region and that's stalling government efforts to tackle deforestation, according to a think tank report Thursday.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kim stresses to North Korean troops they should treat South Korea as a hostile foreign enemy
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un reminded his troops to treat South Korea as a hostile foreign enemy and insisted that the North wouldn’t hesitate to attack its rival if the South infringes upon its sovereignty, state media said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sinwar's removal from battlefield is ‘significant,’ presents opportunity for cease-fire, State Department says
item.description: The State Department on Thursday said the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar presents an "opportunity" for a potential cease-fire and an end to the yearlong war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas leader’s death shakes up Middle East war
item.description: The death of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar has shaken up the war in the Middle East, with Israel now having eliminated the top leaders of the Palestinian militant group, including the officials who plotted the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: US had no role in Israel operation that killed Hamas leader: Pentagon
item.description: U.S. forces were not involved in the Israeli operation that killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar on Thursday, though American intelligence has aided Israel's search for the group's leaders, according to the Pentagon. “This was an Israeli operation. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Kenya's Parliament removes the deputy president from office in an impeachment trial
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Kenya’s Senate voted Thursday to remove Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua from office in an impeachment trial on corruption and other allegations, hours after the deputy president was taken to the hospital with chest pains.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN envoy proposes partitioning Western Sahara
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. envoy to Western Sahara suggested dividing the territory between Morocco and the pro-independence Polisario Front as a way to settle the decades-long conflict.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar killed, Israel says
item.description: Yahya Sinwar, the head of Hamas and the architect of the Oct. 7, 2023, terrorist attack on Israel, was killed Thursday during an Israeli military operation in the Gaza Strip, according to the military.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Former Mexican public security chief gets more than 38 years, $2M fine for taking cartel bribes
item.description: Mexico’s ex-public security secretary Genaro García Luna was sentenced to more than 38 years in prison and a $2 million fine for taking millions in bribes to protect the Sinaloa cartel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea confirmed Thursday that its recently revised constitution defines South Korea as “a hostile state" for the first time, two days after it blew up front-line road and rail links that once connected the country with the South.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aging farmers face extreme temperatures as they struggle to maintain Japan's rice crop
item.description: KAMIMOMI, Japan (AP) — In the remote village of Kamimomi in Japan’s western Okayama prefecture, a small group of rice farmers began their most recent harvest in sweltering heat, two weeks sooner than usual.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA MCGILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel pressured by UN and US to step up action to tackle Gaza's escalating humanitarian crisis
item.description: Israel’s U.N. Ambassador Danny Danon insisted that his country’s humanitarian efforts remain “as comprehensive as ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China's leader makes rare visit to province facing Taiwan following major war exercises
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China’s President Xi Jinping made a rare visit to a province facing Taiwan following military exercises that mobilized China's navy, air force, missile force and land troops to simulate a blockade of the self-governing island democracy that Beijing claims as its own territory a
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taliban begins enforcing rule banning 'images of living beings' in Afghan media
item.description: The Taliban is preventing certain outlets from airing images of human beings or animals, according to officials. The rule pertains to certain provinces at the time being.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Christmas Island National Park travel guide where millions of colorful crabs migrate annually
item.description: From diving to birdwatching, there is a plethora of experiences to take part in at Christmas Island National Park. The red crab migration is one popular experience for tourists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trudeau says Canadian police went public with Indian diplomat allegations to prevent more violence
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Wednesday that Canada's national police force went public with its allegations against Indian diplomats this week because it had to disrupt violent acts in Canada including drive-by-shootings, extortions and even murder.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Biden administration staffers who resigned over Gaza launch PAC
item.description: Two former officials who resigned from the Biden administration in protest over U.S. support for Israel have announced the creation of a political action committee focused on supporting candidates based on policy positions toward the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Italian Senate passes law making it illegal to seek surrogacy abroad
item.description: Italy’s Senate approved legislation Wednesday criminalizing the seeking of surrogacy in other countries by Italian citizens. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni advocated for the policy, which is seen as targeting same-sex couples.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: No slowing of US government contracts for Raytheon amid Qatar bribery charges
item.description: Under the agreement, the company will have to demonstrate good conduct for the next 3½ years and foster a culture of ethics and compliance with anti-corruption laws.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation staff, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine defense minister confident can replenish troops but in need of weapons, equipment from allies
item.description: As the war in Ukraine drags on with no end in sight and mounting casualties, the country desperately needs international assistance and must quickly train and equip new soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Set sail on world’s largest cruise ship, which can accommodate over 7,000 guests per voyage
item.description: Icon of the Seas is the world's largest cruise ship. With extensive dining options, record-breaking pools and breathtaking live shows, there's something for everyone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An Australian police sergeant likely to be charged over a Nazi salute
item.description: An Australian state police chief apologized to the Jewish community after a sergeant allegedly performed a Nazi salute.
item.dc:creator: Rod McGuirk, The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: All about National Archaeological Museum in Athens, one of the world’s most prominent museums of its kind
item.description: The National Archaeological Museum houses artifacts dating back thousands of years. The collections are full of sculptures, artworks and more for guests to explore.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vial linked to UK woman's death had enough poison to kill thousands
item.description: Sturgess and her partner collapsed after they came into contact with a discarded perfume bottle containing the nerve agent.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Palestinians in northern Gaza described heavy Israeli bombardment Saturday in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people, as Israel warned people there and in southern Lebanon to get out of the way of offensives against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine cracks down on alleged draft dodgers
item.description: Ukrainian military recruitment officers raided restaurants, bars and a concert hall in Kyiv, checking military registration documents and detaining men who were not in compliance.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, NewsNation
